steps.title: Use the sign pattern
steps.content: The function $f(x)=(x-a)(x-b)$ opens upward. It is negative between its two roots and positive outside them.

steps.title: Locate the roots
steps.content: The values go positive at $12$, negative at $15$, and positive at $18$. Therefore, one root is between $12$ and $15$, and the other is between $15$ and $18$.

steps.title: Use integer roots
steps.content: Because $a$ and $b$ are integers, the possible roots are $13$ or $14$ for the first interval and $16$ or $17$ for the second interval. The possible sums are $$13+16=29,\quad 13+17=30,\quad 14+16=30,\quad 14+17=31$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Only $29$ appears among the choices, so D is correct.
